elements.text: Information security defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: F
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Critical Data
elements.label: text

elements.text: All information necessary for the Seller/Servicer to perform its
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations under the Seller/Servicer’s Purchase Documents,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including, but not limited to, Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Protected Information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: V
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vulnerability
elements.label: text

elements.text: Management
elements.label: text

elements.text: The process of identifying and testing known software
elements.label: text

elements.text: vulnerabilities within a system and prioritizing remediation
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to each vulnerability’s likelihood of occurrence and how
elements.label: text

elements.text: the exploitation of the vulnerability would impact the system.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Information security minimum requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Information security program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must define an individual or group of individuals responsible for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: development of information security requirements, including the adoption,
elements.label: text

elements.text: implementation, maintenance and administration of written minimum-security standards,
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies and procedures that responsibly address critical issues such as user
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities (e.g., “Acceptable Use”); ownership of and access to information;
elements.label: text

elements.text: baseline security practices; physical, administrative and technical security protection
elements.label: text

elements.text: mechanisms and other requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Not less than annually, Seller/Servicers must review and assess the adequacy of their
elements.label: text

elements.text: information security policies and procedures used in connection with the selling and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing of Freddie Mac Mortgages to ensure compliance with the Guide, their other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents and industry best practices (including as set forth by the National
elements.label: text

elements.text: Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) and International Organization for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Standardization (ISO)/International Electrotechnical Commission (IEC) standards).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon request of Freddie Mac, Seller/Servicers must make their information security
elements.label: text

elements.text: program policies and procedures available; further, upon request of Freddie Mac,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must provide an attestation executed by a duly authorized corporate
elements.label: text

elements.text: officer of the adequacy of these policies and procedures, including following the
elements.label: text

elements.text: termination of a Seller/Servicer’s right to sell or service Mortgages, or the occurrence of
elements.label: text

elements.text: an Incident (as defined in Section 1302.5).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Human resources security
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following are requirements related to human resources security:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Pre-employment screening: Seller/Servicers must conduct, or retain a qualified third
elements.label: text

elements.text: party to conduct, thorough background verification checks (screening) for all
elements.label: text

elements.text: candidates for employment or contractor status who will have access to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information, Protected Information or Systems (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Code of conduct or non-disclosure agreement: Prior to being granted access to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac confidential information, Protected Information or Systems,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must require all employees, contractors and third parties to (i) sign a
elements.label: text

elements.text: non-disclosure agreement or (ii) be subject to a code of conduct, which in either case
elements.label: text

elements.text: includes obligations to restrict the use or disclosure of and to maintain as confidential
elements.label: text

elements.text: all Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Protected Information and information related to or contained in Systems: The code
elements.label: text

elements.text: of conduct must be acknowledged by the employee, contractor or third party, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: must address at least the following subjects:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Appropriate use of company assets
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Information protection, including non-disclosure and confidentiality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Records management
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Information security and privacy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Business courtesies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Personal investments and insider trading
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Conflicts of interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Information security awareness, education and training: At least annually,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must provide information security awareness training to all
elements.label: text

elements.text: employees and contractors who have access to Freddie Mac confidential information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Protected Information and/or Systems.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The training should incorporate current cybersecurity threats, including phishing,
elements.label: text

elements.text: social engineering, supply chain attacks, malware/ransomware, credential
elements.label: text

elements.text: compromise via weak password hygiene, insider threats, artificial intelligence (AI)-
elements.label: text

elements.text: powered tactics (e.g., deepfakes, targeted phishing content) and threats to AI systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e.g., model inversion, data poisoning, prompt injection). When necessary, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: organization personnel and partners must receive AI risk management training to
elements.label: text

elements.text: enable them to perform their duties and responsibilities consistent with related
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies, procedures and agreements. At a minimum, the training must address the
elements.label: text

elements.text: importance of data security, provide details on roles and responsibilities for all users
elements.label: text

elements.text: in protecting information at the Seller/Servicer (along with practical ways to
elements.label: text

elements.text: incorporate information security into daily routines) and include the specific
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes in place for protecting Freddie Mac confidential information and Protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Physical and environmental security controls
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must create and maintain:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A physical security control program of the organization’s buildings and facilities that
elements.label: text

elements.text: contain information systems, designed to detect, monitor and prevent unauthorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: access and to respond to physical security incidents using real-time physical intrusion
elements.label: text

elements.text: alarms and surveillance equipment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An updated list of personnel with authorized access to facilities where information
elements.label: text

elements.text: systems reside, including an access privilege review performed not less than annually
elements.label: text

elements.text: and upon the departure of any authorized personnel
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Environmental controls to monitor, mitigate and protect the organization with regards
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a loss of connectivity, access to or integrity of information and damage caused by
elements.label: text

elements.text: natural disasters or man-made incidents such as fire, earthquake, flood, hurricane,
elements.label: text

elements.text: tornado or weather-related adverse conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A clean desk policy that ensures that Freddie Mac confidential information and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Protected Information are stored securely
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Communications and operations management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must implement technical security measures designed to monitor for,
elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigate against and prevent malicious software, stop unwanted spam and traffic and to
elements.label: text

elements.text: protect against unauthorized use of wireless connections. Measures must include those
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in the remainder of this section or meet industry best practices, whichever is
elements.label: text

elements.text: more stringent.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (v) Data transmission and data loss prevention
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a data loss prevention/transmission protection mechanism and related
elements.label: text

elements.text: written policy establishing requirements to protect the confidentiality and integrity of
elements.label: text

elements.text: information exchange using technology applications or information systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure adequate and up-to-date data loss prevention software is used and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding management process is in place to scan for sensitive information
elements.label: text

elements.text: stored on media and outgoing transmissions over public communication paths as well
elements.label: text

elements.text: as to restrict the transfer of data to USB and other removable media devices at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: desktop level
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not transmit to System(s) or the Uniform Collateral Data Portal®, through an
elements.label: text

elements.text: application programming interface or otherwise, any Malicious Code. “Malicious
elements.label: text

elements.text: Code” means software or firmware intended to perform an unauthorized process that
elements.label: text

elements.text: may have adverse impacts on the confidentiality, integrity, or availability of an
elements.label: text

elements.text: information system (including, without limitation, data in transit), such as a “virus,”
elements.label: text

elements.text: “time bomb,” “worm,” “trojan horse,” or other code-based entity that infects a host;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ransomware, spyware and certain forms of adware are also examples of Malicious
elements.label: text

elements.text: Code.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct regular audits to ensure compliance with data loss prevention policies and
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures and verify that data loss prevention software is functioning as intended
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ For data loss prevention policies, implement a data classification scheme to identify
elements.label: text

elements.text: and categorize Freddie Mac confidential information and personal information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure adequate and up-to-date data loss prevention software is used and a
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding management process is in place to scan all inbound files and e-mails
elements.label: text

elements.text: for malware
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish an incident response plan for data loss prevention policy violations. Ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: staff are trained to respond to potential data loss incidents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vi)Anti-virus program/updates
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must install anti-virus software to protect servers and end user systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: and must keep all such software up to date with the latest anti-virus software and
elements.label: text

elements.text: definitions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (vii) Network security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implement information technology controls such as stateful firewalls to block all
elements.label: text

elements.text: traffic inbound from, and outbound to, public networks that have not been expressly
elements.label: text

elements.text: permitted by policy (i.e., “deny by default”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manage and restrict ports, protocols and services to only those that are required and
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved for business operations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Formally recertify and authorize firewall rules upon each significant change
elements.label: text

elements.text: (including, but not limited to, physical appliance updates, firmware updates and
elements.label: text

elements.text: other changes to firewall technology) in infrastructure and otherwise not less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: annually
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define a network segmentation strategy, commensurate with the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: risk profile, that is documented in policies and procedures and requires physical and
elements.label: text

elements.text: logical segmentation from the user environment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish a comprehensive strategy and process for endpoint detection and response
elements.label: text

elements.text: that includes continuous monitoring of endpoint detection and response and remote
elements.label: text

elements.text: access technologies to detect any misuse or abuse
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least annually, review and update the endpoint detection and response strategy
elements.label: text

elements.text: and processes to adapt to evolving threats and technologies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define criteria and indicators to identify when privileged credentials are
elements.label: text

elements.text: compromised or used maliciously
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Develop procedures for prompt response and mitigation of identified threats,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensuring that all incidents involving compromised credentials are thoroughly
elements.label: text

elements.text: investigated and remediated
elements.label: text

elements.text: (viii) Privacy policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain a written privacy policy that has been approved by
elements.label: text

elements.text: management and communicated to all appropriate personnel. The policy must meet
elements.label: text

elements.text: industry best practices and require the Seller/Servicer to have an easily accessible
elements.label: text

elements.text: online privacy notice that complies with applicable laws.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ix) Mobile computing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain a written mobile device/computing management
elements.label: text

elements.text: (MDM) policy that has been approved by management and communicated to all
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate personnel. This policy must reflect current and best practices, specifying
elements.label: text

elements.text: parameters including but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approved and prohibited applications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cryptographic mechanisms to ensure data security
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identity and access management requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Software updates
elements.label: text

elements.text: (x) Wireless networks
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must control, secure and monitor wireless access points. In addition,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers that offer wireless networks for network users must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implement and keep up to date a strong Wireless Local Area Network (WLAN)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authentication method that meets or exceeds the current industry standard Encryption
elements.label: text

elements.text: strength and technology
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibit use of outdated wireless technologies such as Wired Equivalent Privacy
elements.label: text

elements.text: (WEP)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least annually, perform reviews of approved wireless networks to validate and
elements.label: text

elements.text: verify authorized users and access points
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Password protect and control administrative access to the router
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xi)Vulnerability management and penetration testing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must conduct vulnerability testing on a regular basis and have a process
elements.label: text

elements.text: in place to analyze and remediate identified vulnerabilities. To accomplish this, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not less than annually, employ a qualified and independent third party to conduct
elements.label: text

elements.text: penetration testing on systems or system components used to store, access, process
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or transmit Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected Information or
elements.label: text

elements.text: connect to System(s). At a minimum, the executive summary of the penetration report
elements.label: text

elements.text: on Freddie Mac-related services and data should be made available to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: for review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a written vulnerability assessment process and policy that has been
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved by Senior Management, including, at a minimum, the Chief Information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Officer, Chief Technology Officer, Chief Information Security Office or Chief Risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: Officer (or the equivalents thereof), communicated to appropriate personnel and has
elements.label: text

elements.text: an owner that implements, maintains and reviews the policy at least annually to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that it consistently reflects industry best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ As needed, document a remediation plan and remediate all identified vulnerabilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: per defined service level agreements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a record of all identified vulnerabilities and their remediation status
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xii) Configuration and patch management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implement and maintain a written patch management process and a policy that has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been approved by management, communicated to all appropriate personnel and has
elements.label: text

elements.text: a designated owner that reviews, implements and maintains the policy to ensure that
elements.label: text

elements.text: it consistently reflects industry best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Develop and execute a process for developing and maintaining secure configuration
elements.label: text

elements.text: baselines (also known as hardening guides, baseline secure configurations) of
elements.label: text

elements.text: infrastructure components
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deploy intrusion detection and/or prevention systems (IDS and/or IPS) with
elements.label: text

elements.text: generated events fed into centralized systems for analysis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define, implement and maintain preventive controls designed to block malicious
elements.label: text

elements.text: messages and attachments from entering the environment
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Designate qualified personnel responsible for performing timely software updates
elements.label: text

elements.text: and patches and maintain a process for testing and installing software updates as
elements.label: text

elements.text: they become available
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xiii) Auditing, logging and monitoring
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Develop, implement and maintain written guidelines and requirements for the
elements.label: text

elements.text: logging and monitoring of activities and action within information systems. If the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer uses an enterprise log management function, the subject
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements must be integrated with such log management function. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer may elect to use an external vendor for information security
elements.label: text

elements.text: monitoring, subject to the provisions of this Chapter 1302.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Develop, implement and maintain written log retention and handling requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: to ensure logs retain relevant, useable and timely information sufficient to identify
elements.label: text

elements.text: user access and/or system activities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Perform an independent security assessment of the control environment not less
elements.label: text

elements.text: than annually and upon the occurrence of any Incident (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xiv) Software and application development life cycle (SDLC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller/Servicer develops or acquires any application or software that stores,
elements.label: text

elements.text: accesses, processes or transmits Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information or connects to Systems, the Seller/Servicer must develop, implement and
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintain a written SDLC process and policy that have been approved by management.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This process and policy and all associated procedures collectively must incorporate
elements.label: text

elements.text: current industry best practices, and specifically:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish roles and responsibilities defining who is responsible and accountable for
elements.label: text

elements.text: each phase and step of the development process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Standardize the stages (e.g., planning, analysis, design, development, testing,
elements.label: text

elements.text: deployment, maintenance) for all projects and adhere to separation of non￾production, testing and production environments
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Integrate security practices throughout the process of development (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: DevSecOps) as follows:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Adherence to formal and industry recognized standards must be included.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Where applicable, these standards may include (among others):
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Open Web Application Security Project Top 10,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The NIST Secure Software Development Framework,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ ISO and IEC 27034,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Computer Emergency Response Team Secure Coding, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Computer Information Systems Controls
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ When incorporating open-source code, a dependency inventory Software Bill of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Materials must be documented and maintained
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Vulnerability testing during all phases prior to moving code into production
elements.label: text

elements.text: must be conducted
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish formal quality assurance practices, including rigorous testing during all
elements.label: text

elements.text: phases. At a minimum, Seller/Servicer must perform Dynamic Application Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Testing and/or Static Application Security Testing scans on code and document
elements.label: text

elements.text: results.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish a formal, documented and accountable change management process
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Create and maintain documentation sufficient to ensure compliance with all
elements.label: text

elements.text: relevant statutory, regulatory and internal policies, including all sections of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xv) Data Encryption
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain a formal Encryption and cryptography use policy that has been approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: by Senior Management, including, at a minimum, the Chief Information Officer,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chief Technology Officer, Chief Information Security Office or Chief Risk Officer
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or the equivalents thereof), and which has been communicated to appropriate
elements.label: text

elements.text: personnel and has an owner that implements, maintains and reviews the policy to
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure it consistently reflects industry best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain an encryption solution that enables the recovery of a compromised
elements.label: text

elements.text: database administrator account
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintain encryption solutions that allow privileged administrators to complete
elements.label: text

elements.text: required actions without the ability to decrypt data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure the protection, integrity and confidentiality of Freddie Mac confidential
elements.label: text

elements.text: information and Protected Information using encryption methods while in transit
elements.label: text

elements.text: and at rest
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Deploy cryptography standards that meet or exceed the then-current industry
elements.label: text

elements.text: standard Encryption strength and technology and prohibit use of outdated
elements.label: text

elements.text: technologies
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Generate, exchange, store, use, replace and delete cryptographic keys in a timely
elements.label: text

elements.text: manner to prevent unauthorized access to those keys
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Use Encryption mechanisms on portable end-user devices to protect data if the
elements.label: text

elements.text: hardware (laptop, mobile device, etc.) is lost or stolen
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xvi) Incident management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Develop and maintain, and implement when triggered, an incident response plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: that provides a roadmap for implementing incident response capabilities and defines
elements.label: text

elements.text: the resources and management support needed. The plan must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be approved by Senior Management, including, at a minimum, the Chief
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information Officer, Chief Technology Officer, Chief Information Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Office or Chief Risk Officer (or the equivalents thereof);
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Include a plan, with a clearly defined process, to shut off access to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Systems when an Incident occurs;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ As specified in Section 1302.5, address any security breaches or incidents
elements.label: text

elements.text: involving Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected Information
elements.label: text

elements.text: promptly and effectively;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be tested at a pre-defined periodic frequency, or more frequently, if prudent,
elements.label: text

elements.text: given the circumstances;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be reviewed and updated at least annually
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Document a process to identify and respond to malicious domains, taking
elements.label: text

elements.text: immediate action to block access to these domains across all network and endpoint
elements.label: text

elements.text: security systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Notify relevant stakeholders and provide detailed reports on the identified threats
elements.label: text

elements.text: and actions taken
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Coordinate with Related Third Parties on Incident detection and remediation
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Regularly review and update the response processes to adapt to evolving threats and
elements.label: text

elements.text: technologies, incorporating lessons learned from past incidents to enhance the
elements.label: text

elements.text: overall effectiveness of the response strategy
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Annually, unless formally activated, test the effectiveness of the incident response
elements.label: text

elements.text: plan and capabilities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Annually, unless formally activated, audit the incident response plan. The audit may
elements.label: text

elements.text: be performed by (i) an internal independent function within the organization, or (ii)
elements.label: text

elements.text: an external entity who is qualified to do such audits;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Evaluate lessons learned from all Incidents;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implement or identify an existing classification scale for Incidents to quantify the
elements.label: text

elements.text: severity of the Incident;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have documented action plans for remediation of Incidents, including playbooks
elements.label: text

elements.text: for Incidents related to AI/ML (as defined in Section 1302.8)
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xvii)Access control
elements.label: text

elements.text: A. Access management policy
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish, implement and maintain an access management policy that aligns
elements.label: text

elements.text: with industry best practices, including a process for granting and removing
elements.label: text

elements.text: system access, requirements for Authentication and rules of behavior. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: access management policy must be reviewed and updated at least annually.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define a process for securing administrative user and other privileged accounts,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which must be reviewed and updated at least annually, and enforce access and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Authentication requirements, including establishing and maintaining multifactor
elements.label: text

elements.text: authentication procedures for system administrators and other privileged
elements.label: text

elements.text: accounts
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define and enforce remote access requirements, including acceptable use,
elements.label: text

elements.text: approvals and recertification processes
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define and enforce requirements around locked accounts after multiple failed
elements.label: text

elements.text: login attempts and timeout requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish and enforce access control methods that limit access to systems,
elements.label: text

elements.text: physical or virtual resources and grant access to users, including third parties,
elements.label: text

elements.text: on a need-to-know basis
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Define and enforce requirements for multifactor authentication where applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: (privileged sessions, remote connectivity, applications housing Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information or Protected Information, etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manage user accounts for System(s), in accordance with the Guide and the other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents. Seller/Servicers must monitor account access for users
elements.label: text

elements.text: who transfer roles or are terminated or no longer need access to their accounts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must notify Freddie Mac (see Directory 8) within one Business
elements.label: text

elements.text: Day after any transfer or termination.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to and comply with the instructions to update systems access for relevant
elements.label: text

elements.text: applications at https://sf.freddiemac.com/tools-learning/technology-login
elements.label: text

elements.text: B. Granting, removing and reviewing access
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain and enforce written procedures to ensure that only
elements.label: text

elements.text: necessary personnel are granted access to Freddie Mac Systems. Access should be
elements.label: text

elements.text: granted based on the principle of least privilege, ensuring that individuals have only
elements.label: text

elements.text: the access necessary for their roles. Access to sensitive information is limited to
elements.label: text

elements.text: authorized users only, commensurate with their roles and responsibilities, on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: need-to-know basis. The procedures must specifically address:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Approval of access requests
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Removal of access for terminations and transfers
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Analysis of user access and removal of access that is inactive or no longer
elements.label: text

elements.text: needed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least annual review of all user access privileges and certification of access
elements.label: text

elements.text: according to the minimum information necessary to access permission rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At least twice annual review of all administrator roles and responsibilities and
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification of access according to the minimum necessary to access permission
elements.label: text

elements.text: rules
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Timely compliance with Freddie Mac requests pertaining to recertification of
elements.label: text

elements.text: user access privileges and/or administrator roles for Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Prohibit or prevent using the same service account identifiers and passwords in
elements.label: text

elements.text: both production and non-production environments
elements.label: text

elements.text: C. Authentication requirements and guidelines
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must authenticate employee identity with a multifactor
elements.label: text

elements.text: authentication method or process that includes, but is not limited to, user
elements.label: text

elements.text: identification codes, passwords, personal identification numbers, a smart card
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or a token device. Ensure that this process is fatigue resistant. Ensure that all
elements.label: text

elements.text: access to Freddie Mac data requires multifactor authentication.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers should establish comprehensive procedures to monitor all
elements.label: text

elements.text: enterprise Authentication services and processes. This includes defining criteria and
elements.label: text

elements.text: indicators for detecting credential compromise or anomalous activity. Regular
elements.label: text

elements.text: monitoring and analysis should be conducted to identify and respond to potential
elements.label: text

elements.text: threats promptly. Additionally, third-party access should be continuously reviewed
elements.label: text

elements.text: and updated to ensure compliance with security policies and to mitigate risks.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If passwords are used, the Authentication policy must mandate, and Seller/Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: must enforce, minimum guidelines for password complexity, reuse timelines and
elements.label: text

elements.text: password change timelines.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must utilize password vaults with multifactor authentication and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ensure that no passwords are stored in unsecured files.
elements.label: text

elements.text: D. Asset management
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must implement security measures to protect Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information and Protected Information, including encryption, secure
elements.label: text

elements.text: storage solutions and regular security assessments. Seller/Servicers must ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with all relevant regulations and standards for data protection and
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidentiality.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain an inventory management system to track physical
elements.label: text

elements.text: and software assets, such as end-user technology, servers, network devices, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: corresponding asset ownership. The inventory management system must be
elements.label: text

elements.text: reconciled to actual inventory at least annually to verify all assets are included.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must develop procedures for continuous monitoring of access and
elements.label: text

elements.text: usage of Freddie Mac confidential information and Protected Information to detect
elements.label: text

elements.text: and respond to any unauthorized access or anomalies.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documented procedures must be in place detailing guidelines and requirements for
elements.label: text

elements.text: tracking the removal of assets from a facility.
elements.label: text

elements.text: E. Cloud computing
elements.label: text

elements.text: When a Seller/Servicer consumes or provides cloud services that store, process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: access or transmit Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected Information or
elements.label: text

elements.text: connect to any System, the Seller/Servicer must maintain and comply with policies
elements.label: text

elements.text: and standards for managing cloud computing risks. The policy should address:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Due Diligence: Specify appropriate due diligence responsibilities, governance,
elements.label: text

elements.text: ongoing oversight and monitoring of the cloud service providers’ security
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ System vulnerabilities: Articulate processes and responsibilities to securely
elements.label: text

elements.text: configure cloud systems, provision access, and log and monitor the FM
elements.label: text

elements.text: information assets residing in or being processed in the cloud environment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Create and maintain a process for determining the criteria for replacing or
elements.label: text

elements.text: remediating the services provided by a cloud service provider when it has
elements.label: text

elements.text: suffered an incident.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identity and access management: Define roles for cloud access management,
elements.label: text

elements.text: limiting account privileges, implementing multifactor authentication, frequently
elements.label: text

elements.text: updating and reviewing account access, monitoring activity, and requiring
elements.label: text

elements.text: privileged users to have separate usernames and passwords
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Security controls for sensitive data: Define responsibilities for implementing
elements.label: text

elements.text: controls to safeguard sensitive data limit a malicious actor’s ability to exploit
elements.label: text

elements.text: data during a breach
elements.label: text

elements.text: The cloud policy should be approved by management and communicated to
elements.label: text

elements.text: appropriate personnel, and the Seller/Servicer must designate an owner to maintain
elements.label: text

elements.text: and review the policy to ensure it consistently reflects industry best practices.
elements.label: text

elements.text: F. Vendor risk management program
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must implement a vendor risk management program to formally
elements.label: text

elements.text: evaluate, track and measure third-party risk; to assess its impact on all aspects of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: organization’s business; and to develop compensating controls or other forms of
elements.label: text

elements.text: mitigation to safeguard and protect Freddie Mac confidential information, Protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information and Systems from unauthorized persons, malicious software or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: harmful computer information, commands, codes or programs. The risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: management program must include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: I. Criteria for determining when to replace a Related Third Party that has
elements.label: text

elements.text: experienced or is experiencing an incident and
elements.label: text

elements.text: II. A process to replace the Related Third Party when such criteria are met
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must maintain with all Related Third Parties that store, process,
elements.label: text

elements.text: access or transmit Freddie Mac confidential information or Protected Information a
elements.label: text

elements.text: written agreement that obligates them to comply with Minimum Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: similar to what is outlined within this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (xviii) Third-party security assurance – service organization controls (SOC) 2 Type 2
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: A. SOC 2 Type 2 audit requirement
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers whose total annual Mortgage Servicing portfolio equals or exceeds $150
elements.label: text

elements.text: billion shall, at their own expense, engage an independent, qualified auditing firm
elements.label: text

elements.text: to conduct a SOC 2 Type 2 examination in accordance with the standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: established by the American Institute of Certified Public Accountants (AICPA).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The audit shall be performed at least annually and assess the design and operating
elements.label: text

elements.text: effectiveness of Servicer’s internal controls over a reporting period of no less than
elements.label: text

elements.text: six (6) consecutive months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers whose total annual Mortgage Servicing portfolio is less than $150
elements.label: text

elements.text: billion should, at their own expense, engage an independent, qualified auditing
elements.label: text

elements.text: firm to conduct a SOC 2 Type 2 examination in accordance with the standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: established by the AICPA. The audit should be performed at least annually and
elements.label: text

elements.text: assess the design and operating effectiveness of Servicer’s internal controls over a
elements.label: text

elements.text: reporting period of no less than six (6) consecutive months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: B. Scope of trust services criteria (TSC)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Given the sensitive nature of financial services and applicable regulatory
elements.label: text

elements.text: obligations, the SOC 2 Type 2 assessment shall, at a minimum, include all
elements.label: text

elements.text: systems and networks that store, process or transmit Freddie Mac data and cover
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Security (Required): Controls to safeguard against unauthorized access, use
elements.label: text

elements.text: or modification of systems and data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Availability: Controls to ensure reliable and timely access to financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: systems and services
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Confidentiality: Controls to restrict access to and protect the confidentiality
elements.label: text

elements.text: of non-public financial information and other sensitive data
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Processing Integrity: Controls to ensure that financial data processing is
elements.label: text

elements.text: accurate, complete, timely and authorized
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Privacy: If Vendor collects, processes or stores personally identifiable
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, controls to ensure compliance with applicable privacy laws and
elements.label: text

elements.text: commitments
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac reserves the right to require specific TSC based on the nature of
elements.label: text

elements.text: services provided, risk tiering or regulatory guidance (e.g., Federal Financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: Institutions Examination Council, the Gramm-Leach-Bliley Act, New York
elements.label: text

elements.text: Department of Financial Services Part 500).
elements.label: text

elements.text: C. Delivery and review of reports
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer shall provide Freddie Mac with a full and unredacted copy of the final
elements.label: text

elements.text: SOC 2 Type 2 report, management response and any relevant remediation plans
elements.label: text

elements.text: within thirty (30) days of report issuance. Reports must be made available, upon
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac request, on at least an annual basis or upon material changes to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s control environment. Upon Freddie Mac request, Servicers shall also
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide supporting documentation evidencing control performance relevant to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: services rendered.
elements.label: text

elements.text: D. Submission process
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will contact Servicers to schedule their annual risk assessment.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers will receive a message from Freddie Mac with instructions to upload
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer’s required documentation to the secure portal. Servicers must use the link
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided in the message to access the secure portal and follow the instructions to
elements.label: text

elements.text: upload their SOC 2 Type 2 report, completed questionnaire and any additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: documentation for controls not covered by the report. After submission, Servicers
elements.label: text

elements.text: will receive a confirmation notification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: E. Remediation of deficiencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer must remediate, in a timely manner, all control exceptions or deficiencies
elements.label: text

elements.text: identified in the SOC 2 Type 2 report that could impact the confidentiality,
elements.label: text

elements.text: integrity or availability of Freddie Mac’s data or systems. Servicer shall provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac with a written remediation plan and periodic updates on remediation
elements.label: text

elements.text: progress, including evidence of issue closure.
elements.label: text

elements.text: F. Regulatory cooperation and oversight
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicer acknowledges that Freddie Mac is subject to oversight by financial
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulators and may be required to disclose or validate the security and compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: posture of Related Third Parties. Servicer agrees to reasonably cooperate with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac in responding to regulatory inquiries, examinations and audits and to
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide relevant documentation or assurances upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: G. Subcontractor compliance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicers required to conduct an annual SOC2 Type 2 assessment must ensure
elements.label: text

elements.text: any Related Third Party that processes, stores or transmits Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: proprietary information, data and Protected Information or supports critical
elements.label: text

elements.text: services, also maintains SOC 2 Type 2 compliance (or equivalent audit
elements.label: text

elements.text: frameworks, such as International Organization for Standards 27001 or Payment
elements.label: text

elements.text: Card Industry Data Security Standards, where applicable). Servicer shall provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: such reports to Freddie Mac upon request and maintain adequate oversight of such
elements.label: text

elements.text: parties.
elements.label: text

elements.text: H. Right to audit
elements.label: text

elements.text: In addition to the SOC 2 Type 2 report, Freddie Mac reserves the right to perform
elements.label: text

elements.text: its own assessment or to designate a third-party assessor to evaluate a Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: security and compliance practices, including data handling, financial transaction
elements.label: text

elements.text: controls and Servicer’s business operations.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.3: Business continuity planning (05/11/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business continuity plans (BCPs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Review of BCPs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional policies and procedures to support the BCP
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ BCP education and training
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) BCPs
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must have a written BCP in place to support its ongoing ability to
elements.label: text

elements.text: proactively identify potential threats to, and prevent interruption of, its business operations,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including without limitation in the event of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A natural, climate or manmade disaster
elements.label: text

elements.text: Or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other event which may cause or causes disruption to Seller/Servicer’s business
elements.label: text

elements.text: operations, processes or access to Freddie Mac confidential information, Protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information and Systems (as defined in Section 2401.1(b)), Mortgage files, data and
elements.label: text

elements.text: records
elements.label: text

elements.text: Example: The BCP should be operative to maintain business operations in the event of loss
elements.label: text

elements.text: of personnel, facilities, third party or access to and use of technology, regardless of the cause
elements.label: text

elements.text: of disruption.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must be able to maintain and restore any such information it is required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: retain in accordance with its Purchase Documents, including without limitation, any
elements.label: text

elements.text: information that it is required to retain pursuant to Chapter 1302, following Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: termination of Seller/Servicer’s right to sell and/or service Mortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The BCP should include activation and escalation criteria, roles and responsibilities, recovery
elements.label: text

elements.text: strategies and procedures for all applicable loss scenarios (i.e., personnel, systems, facilities,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and third parties) regardless of cause, and relevant contact information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Review of BCPs
elements.label: text

elements.text: The BCP must be reviewed and updated by the Seller/Servicer at least annually.
elements.label: text

elements.text: More frequent assessments may be warranted by material changes to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The environment, including without limitation, the critical personnel responsible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: BCP processes and functions, facilities, Related Third Parties and technical
elements.label: text

elements.text: infrastructure;
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The volume of Mortgages originated and sold to Freddie Mac or serviced on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business operations or systems occur
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon request by Freddie Mac, including in the event of material changes to the conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: described above, Seller/Servicer shall promptly conduct additional assessments and share the
elements.label: text

elements.text: results with Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Additional policies and procedures to support the BCP
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to the BCP requirements set forth in Chapter 1302, the Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish, maintain and implement policies and procedures which support the BCP. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: BCP and related policies and procedures must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be approved by the Chief Risk Officer (or the equivalent thereof) and in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Seller/Servicer’s corporate governance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Address response, restoration and repair of actual, known disruptions, as well as
elements.label: text

elements.text: prevention of potential disruptions; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be reviewed and updated not less than annually
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not less than annually, conduct:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A formal risk and threat assessment of the organization
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ More frequent assessments may be warranted by material changes to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The environment, including without limitation, the critical personnel responsible
elements.label: text

elements.text: for BCP processes and functions, facilities, Related Third Parties and technical
elements.label: text

elements.text: infrastructure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The volume of Mortgages originated and sold to Freddie Mac or serviced on
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of Freddie Mac; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business operations or systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Upon request by Freddie Mac, including in the event of material changes to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions described above, Seller/Servicer shall promptly conduct additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessments and share the results with Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Testing of the BCP, including recovery of predefined critical business functions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: which at a minimum shall include a stated test objective, scope/success measures,
elements.label: text

elements.text: dependency type (People, Facility, Third-Party, Technology), process/functions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: assumptions and constraints, identified participants, test environment and test results
elements.label: text

elements.text: and associated remediation actions, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A business impact analysis (BIA) of the organization’s critical processes. At a
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum, the BIA shall specify critical systems, Freddie Mac Critical Data, key
elements.label: text

elements.text: locations, key resources (employees), third parties, single points of failure, recovery
elements.label: text

elements.text: time objectives, recovery point objectives and maximum acceptable outages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Implement and maintain business continuity metrics and controls to track and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: determine any need for changes to the BCP
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) BCP education and training
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer BCP education and training requirements include:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicer must provide BCP training to all employees, contractors and third parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: who have responsibility or oversight of BCP functions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The training must be current in substance and reflect up-to-date continuity threats and
elements.label: text

elements.text: recovery strategies which are consistent with industry best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At a minimum, the training must provide details on roles and responsibilities for all users
elements.label: text

elements.text: in executing the BCP and in protecting Freddie Mac confidential information, Protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information and Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any documents, plans or policies, including without limitation the BCP, referenced in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section shall be provided by Seller/Servicer to Freddie Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.4: Disaster recovery plan (01/01/26)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disaster recovery plans (DRPs)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Disaster and event response
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Additional policies and procedures to support the DRP
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ DRP education and training
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) DRPs
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must have a written DRP needed to support its business operations
elements.label: text

elements.text: following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A natural climate
elements.label: text

elements.text: or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Manmade disaster or other event which may cause or causes interruption to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s business operation, processes or access to systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: The DRP should include plans to maintain or restore access to Freddie Mac confidential
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, Protected Information and Systems (as defined in Section 2401.1(b)), including
elements.label: text

elements.text: without limitation to Mortgage files, data and records, operation at an alternate location, if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, and back up communication and contact systems. Seller/Servicers must maintain
elements.label: text

elements.text: a documented process to ensure out of band or immutable backups for all critical Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac data required for processing.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must be able to maintain and restore any such information it is required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: retain in accordance with its Purchase Documents, including without limitation, any
elements.label: text

elements.text: information that it is required to retain pursuant to Chapter 1302, following Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: termination of Seller/Servicer’s right to sell and/or service Mortgages. If Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: BCP is inclusive of provisions that would otherwise be included in a DRP, no separate DRP
elements.label: text

elements.text: is required.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Disaster and event response
elements.label: text

elements.text: Within the DRP or separately, the Seller/Servicer must establish and develop a plan for
elements.label: text

elements.text: disaster and event response and recovery.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The response plan must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identify a formal disaster and event management team which is responsible for, among
elements.label: text

elements.text: other things:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Implementing a documented disaster and event management plan
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Determining whether a disaster or event warrants activating the DRP
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Providing guidance and oversight with respect to the DRP and related policies and
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be reviewed and updated by Seller/Servicer at least annually but more frequent
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessments may be warranted by material changes to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The environment, including without limitation, the critical personnel responsible for
elements.label: text

elements.text: DRP processes and functions, facilities, Related Third Parties and technical
elements.label: text

elements.text: infrastructure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The volume of Mortgages originated and sold to Freddie Mac or Serviced on behalf
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Business operations or systems occur
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon request by Freddie Mac, including in the event of material changes to the conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: described above, Seller/Servicer shall promptly conduct additional assessments and share the
elements.label: text

elements.text: results with Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Additional policies and procedures to support the DRP
elements.label: text

elements.text: With respect to the DRP requirements set forth in Section 1302.4(a) and (b) above, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish, maintain and implement policies and procedures which support the DRP.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The DRP and related policies and procedures must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be approved by the Chief Information Officer, Chief Technology Officer, Chief
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information Security Office or Chief Risk Officer (or the equivalents thereof) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: in accordance with Seller/Servicer’s corporate governance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Address response, restoration and repair of actual, known disruptions, as well as
elements.label: text

elements.text: prevention of potential disruptions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be reviewed and updated not less than annually
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Not less than annually, conduct:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A formal risk and threat assessment of the organization
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ More frequent assessments may be warranted by material changes to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The environment, including without limitation, the critical personnel
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for DRP processes and functions, facilities, Related Third Parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: and technical infrastructure
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The volume of Mortgages originated and sold to Freddie Mac or serviced on
elements.label: text

elements.text: behalf of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Business operations or systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Upon request by Freddie Mac, including in the event of material changes to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: conditions described above, Seller/Servicer shall promptly conduct additional
elements.label: text

elements.text: assessments and share the results with Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Testing of the DRP, including recovery of predefined critical business functions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A business disruption impact analysis of the organization.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) DRP education and training
elements.label: text

elements.text: DRP education and training must include the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicer must provide DRP training to all employees, contractors and third parties
elements.label: text

elements.text: who have responsibility or oversight of DRP functions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The training must be current in substance and reflect up-to-date continuity threats and
elements.label: text

elements.text: restoration strategies which are consistent with industry best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ At a minimum, the training must provide details on roles and responsibilities for all users
elements.label: text

elements.text: in executing the DRP, and in protecting Freddie Mac confidential information, Protected
elements.label: text

elements.text: Information and Systems (as defined in Section 2401.1(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any documents, plans or policies, including without limitation the DRP, referenced in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: section shall be provided by Seller/Servicer to Freddie Mac upon request.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.5: Incident notification and related obligations
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Security-related requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Non-critical Privacy Incident requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Requirements related to limitation, restriction or termination of System access
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Security-related requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: If a Seller/Servicer knows or believes, or has reasonable information from which to know or
elements.label: text

elements.text: believe, or a cybersecurity professional could reasonably conclude from the circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: and available information, that there may have been any unauthorized access to or use or
elements.label: text

elements.text: acquisition of data or computing resources, or any other security related issue that may
elements.label: text

elements.text: compromise the security, confidentiality, availability, integrity or privacy of Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: confidential information or Protected Information (“Incident”); the Seller/Servicer must
elements.label: text

elements.text: comply with the requirements below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Notification to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Immediately notify Freddie Mac of an Incident via
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://privacyportal.onetrust.com/incident-portal/webforms/94b5e41a-aba0-4e51-
elements.label: text

elements.text: ba48-efa19ce560a1/1b25c37a-a280-44f2-b61e-a693a33c7267 (or by such other means
elements.label: text

elements.text: as specified by Freddie Mac) in the event that such Incident causes the Seller/Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: shut down, disable, or otherwise stop using any or part of any system or other technology
elements.label: text

elements.text: (whether or not such system or technology is developed by the Seller/Servicer or by a
elements.label: text

elements.text: third-party and used by the Seller/Servicer) that the Seller/Servicer ordinarily uses in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with its mortgage origination processes or Servicing Mortgages on behalf of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: In all other instances, as soon as possible, but no later than 36 hours after discovering the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Incident, notify Freddie Mac of the Incident via
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://privacyportal.onetrust.com/incident-portal/webforms/94b5e41a-aba0-4e51-
elements.label: text

elements.text: ba48-efa19ce560a1/1b25c37a-a280-44f2-b61e-a693a33c7267 (or by such other means
elements.label: text

elements.text: as specified by Freddie Mac) or by such other means as Freddie Mac may request. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: notification must include the name, phone number and e-mail address of the contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: leading the Incident investigation, any law enforcement agencies involved and all facts
elements.label: text

elements.text: known about the Incident at the time of notification.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Obligation to investigate and remediate
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must promptly investigate, mitigate and remediate the Incident at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s expense, including identifying all Freddie Mac confidential information
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Protected Information affected by the Incident and preventing the continuation and
elements.label: text

elements.text: recurrence of the Incident.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Information to be provided to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: After notifying Freddie Mac and providing initial information about the Incident, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must continue to update Freddie Mac as the investigation progresses, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: as Freddie Mac may reasonably request, with interim status updates, including new
elements.label: text

elements.text: details learned and progress made since the last update, until Freddie Mac is satisfied that
elements.label: text

elements.text: there has been compliance with Applicable Laws and the event giving rise to the Incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: is fully resolved. remediated and closed.
elements.label: text

elements.text: All information should be sent to the location designated by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The information to be provided by the Seller/Servicer includes the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Technical information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Related internal and external investigations
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Risk factors
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Causation factors
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Technical indicators of compromise (e-mail addresses, hash values, IP addresses,
elements.label: text

elements.text: malware code, vector of compromise, etc.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Tactics, techniques, and procedures associated with the Incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Details surrounding the attack methodology
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Timing of the Incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Technical and forensic reports, if available
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Other information that Freddie Mac may reasonably request to assist Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: in evaluating the potential or actual effect of the Incident on Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: infrastructure and impacted Borrowers or employees
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Actions that are being taken to remediate the Incident and its cause, and to protect
elements.label: text

elements.text: individuals, business assets, and Freddie Mac confidential information and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Protected Information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Remediation actions or workarounds or corrections that resolved the incident and
elements.label: text

elements.text: restored service to its best quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Eradication and recovery steps taken
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Postmortem and similar after-action reports generated
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Other details and information concerning the Incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Final incident closure report
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac and Borrower information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Whether, and if so the extent to which, Freddie Mac confidential information or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Protected Information was accessed, taken, or exposed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The nature and details of the information accessed, taken, or exposed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ All facts relevant to actual or potential misuse of the information, including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: likelihood of misuse and, if applicable, how the information was misused
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Whether there is any cyber or other insurance coverage for expenses related to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Potential damage estimates associated with the Incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance information
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Actions that are being taken to comply with Applicable Laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A Certificate of Compliance (in form and substance requested by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: evidencing, among other things, that the Seller/Servicer has, with respect to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: incident, complied with applicable Federal, State, and local data breach
elements.label: text

elements.text: notification laws and regulations and all Purchase Documents, including without
elements.label: text

elements.text: limitation, the Guide)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Copies of any communications to Borrowers, State and federal agencies and
elements.label: text

elements.text: offices, regulators, credit reporting agencies or others
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Compliance with law
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must comply in a timely manner with Applicable Laws (as defined in
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 1301.2). Where an Incident creates an obligation to notify Borrowers, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer will first give Freddie Mac the opportunity to review and comment on any
elements.label: text

elements.text: notification that in any way refers to or identifies Freddie Mac directly or indirectly.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must comply with Applicable Laws that require notification to
elements.label: text

elements.text: federal or state authorities. Promptly following a request by Freddie Mac, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer will provide Freddie Mac and its designees all information and assistance
elements.label: text

elements.text: needed to enable Freddie Mac to evaluate the need for, and to timely make, any
elements.label: text

elements.text: notification it deems necessary or advisable concerning the Incident.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Non-Critical Privacy Incident requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding the notification requirement set forth in Section 1302.5(a) above, if a
elements.label: text

elements.text: privacy-related Incident affects, or has the potential to affect fewer than 10 Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers (“Non-critical Privacy Incident”), a Seller/Servicer is required to respond to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Incident in accordance with all Applicable Laws, but a Seller/Servicer is not required to
elements.label: text

elements.text: report such Non-critical Privacy Incident to Freddie Mac within the 36-hour reporting
elements.label: text

elements.text: window referenced above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instead, Seller/Servicers must report Non-critical Privacy Incidents to Freddie Mac on a
elements.label: text

elements.text: quarterly basis via https://privacyportal.onetrust.com/incident￾portal/webforms/94b5e41a-aba0-4e51-ba48-efa19ce560a1/7aa89b26-8f28-4ed8-ae19-
elements.label: text

elements.text: 944470d088c9. Seller/Servicers must submit such reports to Freddie Mac by the 5th day of
elements.label: text

elements.text: each January, April, July and October, in each case covering Non-critical Privacy Incidents
elements.label: text

elements.text: in the three immediately preceding calendar months.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An Incident that involves any of the factors listed below is not a Non-critical Privacy
elements.label: text

elements.text: Incident, and a Seller/Servicer must handle the Incident in accordance with Section 1302.5(a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: above:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. The Incident could lead to the compromise of a user account or system providing access
elements.label: text

elements.text: to any Freddie Mac System (as defined in Section 2401.1(b))
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. A malicious actor caused the Incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. The impacted States/territories or federal statute require the Seller/Servicers to notify
elements.label: text

elements.text: State or federal regulators or the affected Borrowers
