elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. There is active or anticipated media coverage of the Incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Law enforcement has been or will be contacted regarding the Incident
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. The Seller/Servicer receives notice from a regulator that it is not or may not be compliant
elements.label: text

elements.text: with its breach response obligations
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. The Seller/Servicer is aware of or reasonably should anticipate material risk to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrowers, investors, Freddie Mac (including without limitation, Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: infrastructure) or others, based on specific facts and circumstances
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Requirements related to limitation, restriction or termination of System access
elements.label: text

elements.text: Whether in connection with the actual or suspected presence of Malicious Code (as defined
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 1302.2), an Incident, or otherwise, Freddie Mac reserves the right, in its sole and
elements.label: text

elements.text: absolute discretion, at any time with or without notice, to limit, restrict and/or terminate a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s access to any System or the UCDP®, temporarily or permanently.
elements.label: text

elements.text: If, and when, Freddie Mac determines that restoring any level of System or the UCDP access
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a Seller/Servicer is appropriate, as a condition to such access restoration, Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: shall provide to Freddie Mac upon request (i) such assurances and information as Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac may deem necessary, in its sole and absolute discretion; and (ii) an attestation, executed
elements.label: text

elements.text: by a duly authorized corporate officer, of the adequacy of any applicable containment,
elements.label: text

elements.text: eradication or remediation of any vulnerability related to such Malicious Code, Incident (as
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined above), and the eradication of any threat actor from the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: environment or any system or technology used by the Seller/Servicer (whether or not such
elements.label: text

elements.text: system or technology is developed by the Seller/Servicer or by a third-party and used by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac shall have no liability to any Seller/Servicer or third party arising out of, related
elements.label: text

elements.text: to, or in connection with Freddie Mac’s limitation, restriction, or termination of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s access to any System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.6: Document retention and destruction (03/11/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must have written data retention and destruction policies and procedures which
elements.label: text

elements.text: contain minimum requirements to comply with applicable corporate, regulatory and legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: standards. The policies and procedures must include the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Identification or definition of the electronic or other information which are subject to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies, including how to handle electronic or other information that is, or may be, subject to
elements.label: text

elements.text: a legal or litigation-related hold
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A data storage, retention and destruction schedule
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Clearly defined criteria for destruction of electronic or other information, regardless of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: form in which the information is stored
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Destruction methodology, including a process for logging and certifying such destruction has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been completed
elements.label: text

elements.text: When electronic or other information is destroyed in accordance with Seller/Servicer’s corporate
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies in the ordinary course, or at Freddie Mac’s direction, such information must be rendered
elements.label: text

elements.text: unreadable and incapable of being re-created. Paper records must be properly and securely
elements.label: text

elements.text: destroyed, and Seller/Servicer must retain evidence of destruction. Upon request,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers will provide to Freddie Mac certificates of destruction or other evidence
elements.label: text

elements.text: demonstrating the fact, time and manner of destruction, be it electronic, paper, hard drive or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other media, which contained the destroyed information. Such certification or evidence is in
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition to any other obligations that Seller/Servicer may have with respect to the destroyed
elements.label: text

elements.text: information, including without limitation pursuant to Section 1301.3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.7: Information security and other requirements
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: for Related Third Parties (03/11/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Require any Related Third Party to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Comply with requirements substantially similar to those imposed on the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: under Chapter 1302
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Refrain from interfering with or impairing any obligations of the Seller/Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, under a Purchase Document or elsewhere, of which any member of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Related Third Party’s Senior Management has actual knowledge
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Designate Freddie Mac as an express, intended third-party beneficiary of each agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: with a Related Third Party, a breach of which may have a Material Adverse Effect and
elements.label: text

elements.text: include in the agreement the obligations referenced above.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, the third-party designation requirement shall not apply to
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreements with any of the Seller/Servicer’s counterparties that have direct relationship(s)
elements.label: text

elements.text: with Freddie Mac relative to the same subject matter and Freddie Mac has direct standing to
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforce the terms of the subject agreement. (MIs, credit bureaus or Warehouse Lenders, for
elements.label: text

elements.text: example, may qualify as such counterparties.) In determining whether the third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: designation requirement applies to a specific counterparty, the Seller/Servicer shall seek
elements.label: text

elements.text: clarification from Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Indemnify Freddie Mac and its directors, officers, employees, agents, successors and assigns
elements.label: text

elements.text: and hold each harmless from and against any and all liabilities, losses, claims, actions,
elements.label: text

elements.text: damages, including, but not limited to, indirect, incidental, special or consequential damages,
elements.label: text

elements.text: whether foreseeable or not, judgments, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys’
elements.label: text

elements.text: fees, arising directly or indirectly out of or relating to any breach of a Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation, warranty, covenant and/or obligation under Chapter 1302, whether such
elements.label: text

elements.text: breach arises out of its own action or inaction or the action or inaction of a Related Third
elements.label: text

elements.text: Party or of any Seller/Servicer or Related Third Party director, officer, employee,
elements.label: text

elements.text: subcontractor, partner, principal, agent, successor or assign. Freddie Mac shall provide the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer with notice of any such claim after it comes to Freddie Mac’s attention.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1302.8: Use of artificial intelligence and machine
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: learning (03/03/26)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Compliance with applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Indemnification
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Governance framework
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Compliance with applicable law
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers that use artificial intelligence and/or machine learning (together, “AI/ML”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: in connection with the origination of Mortgages sold to or guaranteed by Freddie Mac or
elements.label: text

elements.text: Servicing Mortgages on behalf of Freddie Mac must ensure compliance with Applicable Law
elements.label: text

elements.text: and their Purchase Documents. In addition, such use is conditioned upon:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicer’s development, implementation and maintenance of policies and
elements.label: text

elements.text: procedures for the use of AI/ML, which must at a minimum:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be approved by Senior Management, including, at a minimum, the Chief Information
elements.label: text

elements.text: Officer, Chief Technology Officer, Chief Information Security Office or Chief Risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: Officer (or the equivalents thereof)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Be communicated to appropriate personnel who have job responsibility in areas that
elements.label: text

elements.text: use AI/ML; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Have an owner(s) that implements, maintains and reviews the policies and procedures
elements.label: text

elements.text: at least annually to ensure they comply with Applicable Law and consistently reflect
elements.label: text

elements.text: industry best practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Upon request by Freddie Mac, Seller/Servicer’s prompt disclosure of the types of AI/ML
elements.label: text

elements.text: used, the purpose and manner for such use, safeguards to mitigate risks related to the use
elements.label: text

elements.text: of AI/ML, and such other information as Freddie Mac may require.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Indemnification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer agrees to indemnify Freddie Mac and its directors, officers, employees,
elements.label: text

elements.text: agents, successors and assigns, and to hold each harmless from and against any and all
elements.label: text

elements.text: liabilities, losses, claims, actions, damages, including, but not limited to, indirect, incidental,
elements.label: text

elements.text: special or consequential damages, whether foreseeable or not, judgments, costs and expenses,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including reasonable attorneys’ fees, arising directly or indirectly out of or relating to its use
elements.label: text

elements.text: of AI/ML. Freddie Mac shall provide the Seller/Servicer with notice of any such claim after
elements.label: text

elements.text: it comes to Freddie Mac’s attention.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Governance framework
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must establish clear governance frameworks for AI/ML adoption by ensuring
elements.label: text

elements.text: the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Policies, processes, procedures and practices across the organization related to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: mapping, measuring and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent and implemented
elements.label: text

elements.text: effectively
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed and
elements.label: text

elements.text: documented
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The characteristics of trustworthy AI are integrated into organizational policies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes, procedures and practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Processes, procedures and practices are in place to determine the level needed of risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: management activities based on the organization’s risk tolerance
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must do the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assess AI/ML for specific threats such as data poisoning and adversarial inputs
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct regular internal and external audits to identify any potential vulnerabilities or
elements.label: text

elements.text: deviations from established policies. Ongoing monitoring and periodic review of the risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: management process and its outcomes must be planned and organizational roles and
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsibilities clearly defined, including determining the frequency of periodic review
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Regularly monitor AI systems for performance, security breaches and biases
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Conduct audits to ensure compliance with standards like National Institutes of Standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: and Technology 800-53 and International Organization for Standardization 27001
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Establish clear policies and codes of conduct to ensure the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The characteristics of trustworthy AI are integrated into organizational policies,
elements.label: text

elements.text: processes, procedures and practices
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Processes, procedures and practices are in place to determine the needed level of risk
elements.label: text

elements.text: management activities based on the organization’s risk tolerance
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The risk management process and its outcomes are established through transparent
elements.label: text

elements.text: policies, procedures and other controls based on organizational risk priorities
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Apply segregation of duties to prevent conflicts of interest and ensure the following:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Accountability structures are in place so that the appropriate teams and individuals
elements.label: text

elements.text: are empowered, responsible and trained for mapping, measuring and managing AI
elements.label: text

elements.text: risks
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Roles and responsibilities and lines of communication related to mapping, measuring
elements.label: text

elements.text: and managing AI risks are documented and are clear to individuals and teams
elements.label: text

elements.text: throughout the organization
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 1401: Electronic Transactions
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: 1401.1: Overview and defined terms (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Electronic Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: This chapter sets forth the requirements for Electronic Transactions, as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401.1(b). The Electronic Transactions permitted or required hereunder are Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Transactions, as such term is defined in Section 1401.1(b). See Chapter 1402 for
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s selling and Servicing requirements for Mortgages with Electronic Notes
elements.label: text

elements.text: (eNotes).
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer who wishes to sell and/or service eMortgages (as defined in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1402.1(b)) for Freddie Mac should contact its Freddie Mac account representative or the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac eMortgage Team (eMortgage_Team@FreddieMac.com) to begin the process
elements.label: text

elements.text: of determining its eligibility to sell and/or service eMortgages for Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following defined terms are used throughout Chapter 1401, other chapters of the Guide
elements.label: text

elements.text: and the Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents for all Eligible Electronic Transactions.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any defined term in Section 1401.1(b) that is used in the definition of another defined term
elements.label: text

elements.text: in Section 1401.1(b) will be in quotes (“ “) unless the defined term is in a title.
elements.label: text

elements.text: As used in this chapter, the following terms have the meanings ascribed to them below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: Defined terms related to Electronic Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: C
elements.label: text

elements.text: Closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: All loan documents (excluding eNotes) provided to a Borrower at the
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing (settlement) of a Mortgage, as required by (i) federal, State
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or local law, regulations, rules or ordinances, (ii) the originating
elements.label: text

elements.text: lender, and (iii) Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Requirements applicable to all Eligible Electronic Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The following requirements and applicable requirements in Chapter 1302 apply to all
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Electronic Transactions that Freddie Mac permits or requires the Seller/Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: engage in or conduct:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ This Chapter 1401
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other Guide chapters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any user agreement(s) by and between the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac. (Examples
elements.label: text

elements.text: of some Eligible Electronic Transactions include, but are not limited to, use of Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Selling Advisor®, Loan Product Advisor® and Servicing Tools.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: If these requirements conflict with requirements in any of the following, then the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in the other Guide chapters, the Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents or
elements.label: text

elements.text: any user agreement(s) shall control and prevail over these requirements, but only to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: extent necessary to resolve the conflict:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other Guide chapters
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any user agreement(s) by and between the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer believes there is any such conflict, the Seller/Servicer should contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac to discuss any such conflict in an effort to resolve it.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Consent and agreement to engage in and conduct Eligible Electronic Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: When the Seller/Servicer engages in or conducts Eligible Electronic Transactions with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer agrees:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Seller/Servicer consents and agrees to engage in or conduct Eligible Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transactions with Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ To adopt as its signature (among other Electronic Signatures the Seller/Servicer adopts,
elements.label: text

elements.text: from time to time) the Seller/Servicer’s Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer number(s), a copy or
elements.label: text

elements.text: representation of the Seller/Servicer’s representative’s written signature and/or Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Signature and/or such other symbols or processes as expressly required or permitted by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac (“Seller/Servicer’s Electronic Signature(s)”)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That Freddie Mac can rely on any paper Records (with or without Electronic Signatures)
elements.label: text

elements.text: or Electronic Records (with or without Electronic Signatures) that are delivered to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Seller/Servicer will be bound by all Eligible Electronic Transactions the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer engages in or conducts with Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ That the Seller/Servicer will be bound by all Eligible Electronic Transactions that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer engages in or conducts with Freddie Mac based upon telephone
elements.label: text

elements.text: conversations, including those telephone conversations recorded by Freddie Mac in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: ordinary course of business; however, under no circumstances will Freddie Mac be bound
elements.label: text

elements.text: by any telephone conversations recorded by the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will provide the Seller/Servicer with, or require the Seller/Servicer to create, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Confidential Means of Access to Freddie Mac’s Systems or other systems in connection with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Electronic Transactions with Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: A breach of security by the Seller/Servicer (or any of the Seller/Servicer’s Related Third
elements.label: text

elements.text: Parties) with respect to the Seller/Servicer’s Confidential Means of Access to Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Systems may result in unauthorized persons (or authorized persons with malicious or
elements.label: text

elements.text: unlawful intentions) gaining access to Freddie Mac’s Systems. Such persons may:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Destroy, damage or misappropriate Freddie Mac property, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Introduce malicious software (“Malware”) or other harmful computer information,
elements.label: text

elements.text: commands, codes or programs (“Harmful Software”) into Freddie Mac’s Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: Any such unlawful entry or intrusion into Freddie Mac’s Systems may result in substantial
elements.label: text

elements.text: harm to Freddie Mac, including, without limitation, damage to Freddie Mac’s Systems,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Records and/or data or theft of confidential proprietary information and/or consumer
elements.label: text

elements.text: personal private financial information.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer is responsible for protecting and safeguarding the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Confidential Means of Access to Freddie Mac’s System and any Related Third Party or other
elements.label: text

elements.text: third-party System, if applicable, from any and all malicious conduct or theft by any of its
elements.label: text

elements.text: employees, vendors, independent contractors, agents or third parties, unauthorized persons,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Malware and Harmful Software that may:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Enable unauthorized access to Freddie Mac’s Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Cause or facilitate the destruction, corruption, malfunction or appropriation of, or damage
elements.label: text

elements.text: or change to, any of Freddie Mac’s Systems or any Electronic Records and data (e.g.,
elements.label: text

elements.text: financial information, proprietary information, confidential information or personal
elements.label: text

elements.text: private information) stored in Freddie Mac’s Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: Refer to Section 1302.2 for information regarding Freddie Mac’s minimum information
elements.label: text

elements.text: security program requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must notify Freddie Mac immediately in the event the Seller/Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Sustains a breach of security in connection with the Seller/Servicer’s Confidential Means
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Access to Freddie Mac’s Systems, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Becomes aware of the loss, theft or unauthorized disclosure or use of the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Confidential Means of Access to Freddie Mac’s Systems or other Systems, or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Has any reason to believe that the Seller/Servicer’s Confidential Means of Access to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s Systems may be, has been or will be compromised and is no longer safe or
elements.label: text

elements.text: secure
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer’s compliance with Freddie Mac’s requirements in Section 1302.2 will not
elements.label: text

elements.text: relieve the Seller/Servicer from any liability set forth in this chapter; however, adopting and
elements.label: text

elements.text: maintaining such minimum security standards should assist the Seller/Servicer in managing
elements.label: text

elements.text: and mitigating the Seller/Servicer’s security risks. The Seller/Servicer will be solely
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for implementing and updating its security standards and assessing whether the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer should adopt more rigorous minimum security standards.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer’s failure to adopt, implement, maintain, administer and update the
elements.label: text

elements.text: required minimum security standards may result in termination of the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: access to any or all of Freddie Mac’s Systems. In addition, Freddie Mac may take other
elements.label: text

elements.text: actions available under the Guide, the Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents, any user
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement or law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: implemented the minimum security standards (or higher standards) before the TPP may

elements.text: conduct any Eligible Electronic Transactions for or on behalf of (or in connection with

elements.text: doing business with) the Seller/Servicer

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer’s TPPs’ adoption of and compliance with the minimum security
elements.label: text

elements.text: standards (or higher standards) in Section 1401.2(c) will not relieve the Seller/Servicer from
elements.label: text

elements.text: any liability set forth in this chapter; however, requiring the Seller/Servicer’s TPPs to adopt
elements.label: text

elements.text: and maintain such minimum security standards (or higher standards) should assist the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer and its TPPs to manage and mitigate the Seller/Servicer’s and the TPP’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: security risks.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer will be solely responsible for:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Updating and implementing its minimum security standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Assessing whether the Seller/Servicer should adopt (and require its TPPs to adopt) the
elements.label: text

elements.text: security practices recommended by the Seller/Servicer’s federal regulator or even higher
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum security standards
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer’s failure to require its TPPs to adopt, implement, maintain, administer
elements.label: text

elements.text: and update the required minimum security standards (or higher standards) may result in:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Termination of the Seller/Servicer’s access to any or all of Freddie Mac’s Systems and/or
elements.label: text

elements.text: applications
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Other actions taken by Freddie Mac available under the Guide, the Seller/Servicer’s other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents or law
elements.label: text

elements.text: (e) The Seller/Servicer’s due diligence requirements for the Seller/Servicer’s Systems used
elements.label: text

elements.text: to engage in or conduct Eligible Electronic Transactions
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must conduct due diligence reviews on the Seller/Servicer’s Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: before the Seller may engage in or conduct any Eligible Electronic Transactions with Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac to confirm that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer’s Systems are in compliance with the minimum security standards as
elements.label: text

elements.text: described in Section 1401.2(c)
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer’s Systems create valid, enforceable and effective Records, Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Records and Electronic Signatures in compliance with E-SIGN and/or the UETA, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When conducting an Electronic Transactions in connection with a consumer, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s system(s) shall electronically create and permit each such consumer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: electronically sign a Consumer Consent Form after providing each such consumer with
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Transaction disclosures and conducting the reasonable demonstration test in
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance with the applicable requirements of E-SIGN and/or the UETA
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer’s Systems comply with all State and federal laws
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer’s confirmation process would include having the Seller/Servicer’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: Systems reviewed by internal or external computer technology and security experts and legal
elements.label: text

elements.text: experts.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (f) The Seller/Servicer’s due diligence requirements for TPP’s Systems used for Eligible
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Transactions

elements.text: Systems the TPPs intend to use before the Seller/Servicer may permit its TPPs to conduct
elements.label: text

elements.text: Eligible Electronic Transactions for, or on behalf of, the Seller/Servicer (or in connection
elements.label: text

elements.text: with doing business with the Seller/Servicer).
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must use its reasonable and prudent judgment in determining whether its
elements.label: text

elements.text: TPPs have conducted sufficient and appropriate due diligence on the TPP’s Systems. The
elements.label: text

elements.text: TPPs must conduct the same level of due diligence required of the Seller/Servicer in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1401.2(e). The Seller/Servicer will be held responsible for the acts, errors and omissions of
elements.label: text

elements.text: its TPPs and the TPPs’ Systems.

elements.text: 1401.3: Electronic Signatures and Electronic Loan
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Documents (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains information related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Records and Electronic Signatures
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Electronic Loan Documents – Retail Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible Electronic Loan Documents – Wholesale Home Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Recording of paper Closing Documents and post-closing documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Recording of Electronic Closing Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronically notarized (in person or remote online) Electronic documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Remote Ink Notarization
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Electronic Transactions conducted between Servicer and Borrower
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Electronic Records and Electronic Signatures
elements.label: text

elements.text: Without limiting the definition of an Electronic Record set forth above, the term includes, by
elements.label: text

elements.text: way of illustration only:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A facsimile (“fax”) machine copy of a paper Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A fax machine copy of an Electronic Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An e-mail
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ An e-mail with Electronic Record(s) attached
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A scanned copy of a paper Record or Electronic Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A paper Record converted into an Electronic Record
elements.label: text

elements.text: Unless otherwise provided in the Guide or Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents, an
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Signature includes an Electronic copy or representation of a written signature or
elements.label: text

elements.text: other Electronic signing process (permitted or required by Freddie Mac) that is attached to or
elements.label: text

elements.text: logically associated with a paper Record or Electronic Record.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Eligible Electronic Loan Documents – Retail Mortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Federally Regulated Seller/Servicer may sell Retail Mortgages to Freddie Mac that are
elements.label: text

elements.text: originated using Electronic Loan Documents, excluding Mortgages with Electronic Notes
elements.label: text

elements.text: (eNotes), set forth in Exhibit 7, Electronic Loan Documents, provided that the Seller:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Is and remains at all times a Federally Regulated Seller
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adopts, implements, manages, maintains, administers and updates minimum security
elements.label: text

elements.text: standards for its Systems
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Adopts, implements, manages, maintains and administers an initial (and annually
elements.label: text

elements.text: thereafter) due diligence and certification process to certify that the Seller’s System
elements.label: text

elements.text: complies with the Seller’s minimum security standards and E-SIGN and/or the UETA, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintains Records of its initial and annual due diligence and certification process so that
elements.label: text

elements.text: such information may be reviewed by Freddie Mac at any time
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Represents and warrants to, and covenants with, Freddie Mac that each Electronic Loan
elements.label: text

elements.text: Document the Seller/Servicer creates and uses in the origination and closing processes (i)
elements.label: text

elements.text: complies with all applicable federal and/or State laws and regulations, and (ii) is valid,
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforceable and effective in accordance with the terms therein
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Stores all such Electronic Loan Documents in accordance with the requirements of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Guide. If there is a Transfer of Servicing, the Seller must securely transfer all such
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Loan Documents to the Transferee Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Eligible Electronic Loan Documents – Wholesale Home Mortgages

elements.text: Chapter 1402: Freddie Mac eMortgage
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: Requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1402.1: Introduction to eMortgages; Seller/Servicer
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: and Product Eligibility (12/17/25)
elements.label: text

elements.text: An eMortgage is a Mortgage that is originated using an eNote. The Security Instrument and other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage documents may be paper or Electronic Records.
elements.label: text

elements.text: This section contains:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Defined terms
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Seller/Servicer eligibility and approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Eligible eMortgage types and products
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Introduction
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 1402 sets forth Freddie Mac’s requirements for eMortgages. Seller/Servicers,
elements.label: text

elements.text: custodians and System Providers must comply with these requirements to create, store, sell
elements.label: text

elements.text: and service eMortgages. The requirements and specifications in this chapter are based, for
elements.label: text

elements.text: the most part, on the requirements of E-SIGN and the UETA.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Interpreting requirements set forth in this Chapter 1402
elements.label: text

elements.text: Most types of Mortgages are eligible for delivery to Freddie Mac as eMortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgages that are not currently eligible for sale to Freddie Mac as eMortgages are set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in Section 1402.1(d).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers of eMortgages must comply with all selling and Servicing requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: of the Guide and the Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents, as applicable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: including the special requirements set forth in this chapter.
elements.label: text

elements.text: With the exception of a Seller/Servicer’s obligation to comply with applicable law, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements set forth in this chapter prevail over conflicting or contradictory
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements in other chapters of the Guide or the Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents with respect to any eMortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Complying with eMortgage requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer must obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval, which it may provide
elements.label: text

elements.text: in its sole discretion, to sell to and/or service eMortgages for Freddie Mac. This approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: is a Purchase Document and may include additional restrictions, requirements, conditions
elements.label: text

elements.text: or stipulations relating to the sale and/or Servicing of eMortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicers of eMortgages must ensure that they and their System Providers (and if
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, Correspondents and/or Mortgage Brokers) comply with the requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: specified in this chapter. In addition, contracts between the Seller/Servicer and any
elements.label: text

elements.text: System Providers (and if applicable, Correspondents and/or Mortgage Brokers) regarding
elements.label: text

elements.text: the provision of eMortgage closing services and eNote Vault System services, must
elements.label: text

elements.text: contain certain terms and conditions that may be prescribed by Freddie Mac in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval or other Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer is solely, and, if applicable, with any Servicing Agent are jointly,
elements.label: text

elements.text: responsible for any failure to comply with the provisions of the MERS® eRegistry
elements.label: text

elements.text: membership agreement, including, but not limited to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any rules and procedures; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Any liability that it, or its applicable Servicing Agent, or Freddie Mac incurs as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: result of Seller/Servicer’s registration of (or failure to timely register) eNotes in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: MERS eRegistry
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Transfer of Control identifying Freddie Mac as the Controller of an eNote registered in
elements.label: text

elements.text: the MERS eRegistry does not relieve the Seller/Servicer and, if applicable, any Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent, from its responsibility to comply with all applicable provisions of the MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: eRegistry rules, the Guide, and the Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Seller/Servicer eligibility and approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: To sell eMortgages to Freddie Mac, a Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Contact its Freddie Mac account representative to determine eligibility; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain Freddie Mac’s prior written approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s approval of a Seller/Servicer to sell eMortgages does not necessarily mean
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval to service eMortgages for Freddie Mac or vice versa. The approval process to
elements.label: text

elements.text: service eMortgages is detailed in Section 1402.5(a)(i).
elements.label: text

elements.text: Before delivering an eMortgage to Freddie Mac, an approved Seller/Servicer of eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: must either:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be approved as a Servicer of eMortgages; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Be approved to engage in a Concurrent Transfer of Servicing involving eMortgages to a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Transferee Servicer who has been approved to service eMortgages for Freddie Mac (see
elements.label: text

elements.text: Section 1402.5(e)(i))
elements.label: text

elements.text: (d) Eligible eMortgage types and products
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon obtaining Freddie Mac’s written approval to sell eMortgages to Freddie Mac and

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer must ensure that the Systems comply with the information security and

elements.text: Encryption Standards ■ Uses encryption algorithms compliant with National
elements.label: text

elements.text: Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS)
elements.label: text

elements.text: 140-2 guidance, as may be amended, updated or
elements.label: text

elements.text: superseded by subsequent NIST FIPS guidance

elements.text: ■ TLS sessions that use a minimum of 2048-bit
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (RSA) key and 128-bit AES key
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Data must be encrypted both while in transit and at
elements.label: text

elements.text: rest
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: TLS/SSL session authentication which support a
elements.label: text

elements.text: minimum SHA-256 signing hash

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: Continuity
elements.label: text

elements.text: Has disaster recovery, business continuity, redundancy,

elements.text: 1402.3: Delivering eMortgages (12/17/25)
elements.label: section_header

elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ eMortgage delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) eMortgage delivery requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: To deliver an eMortgage to Freddie Mac, a Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Ensure, and represent and warrant to Freddie Mac, that it has complied with all applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: E-SIGN requirements with respect to Borrowers who are “consumers” (as that term is
elements.label: text

elements.text: defined by E-SIGN), as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Close the eMortgage with the applicable Fannie Mae/Freddie Mac Uniform Security
elements.label: text

elements.text: Instrument. See Exhibit 5A for mandatory eNote language to be used with the applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Provide the Borrower at closing with a paper copy of the executed eNote and all other
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mortgage documents, unless the Borrower expressly agrees, in writing or electronically,
elements.label: text

elements.text: to receive an Electronic copy of the eNote and all other Mortgage File Documents and
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide copies of the eNote and other Mortgage File Documents, if requested by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower in the future.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Register an eNote with the MERS® eRegistry, using a MIN, as soon as possible, but no
elements.label: text

elements.text: later than one (1) Business Day after the eMortgage loan closing date and before
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification of the eMortgage by Freddie Mac as set forth in Section 1402.3(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: 5. Deliver the Authoritative Copy of the eNote, using MERS eDelivery, to Freddie Mac’s or
elements.label: text

elements.text: a Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian’s eNote Vault System. Upon receipt
elements.label: text

elements.text: of an eNote, the eNote Vault System will validate (re-compute) the eNote’s Tamper￾Seals and compare them with the corresponding Tamper-Seals in the MERS eRegistry.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: The list of Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodians can be found at
elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/working-with-us/electronic-loan-documents/emortgage￾ready-participants#enote-custodians. If your Document Custodian is not currently
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved for eNote custody and is interested in eNote custody, please refer them to
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage_Team@FreddieMac.com.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 6. Retain a Copy of the Authoritative Copy of the eNote in Seller/Servicer’s eNote Vault
elements.label: text

elements.text: System.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 7. Send an offer of Transfer of Control of the eNote to Freddie Mac and Location of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote to Freddie Mac or a Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian, as
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable, using the MERS eRegistry. For eMortgages delivered through Cash-Released
elements.label: text

elements.text: Xchange®, an offer of Transfer of Control and Transfer of Location must be sent to
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 8. Deliver ULDD Data Point eNote Indicator (Sort ID 233) as “true” in Loan Selling
elements.label: text

elements.text: Advisor® to identify the Mortgage as an eMortgage. Also, deliver ULDD Data Point
elements.label: text

elements.text: Remote Online Notarization Indicator (Sort ID 398.2) as “true” if the eMortgage loan is
elements.label: text

elements.text: closed using the Remote Online Notarization process.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) eNote certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac will not purchase an eMortgage delivered by a Seller/Servicer to Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: until Freddie Mac or a Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian has certified that
elements.label: text

elements.text: the eNote data matches that data delivered in Loan Selling Advisor. Any other certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: arrangement must be expressly approved by Freddie Mac.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may agree to provide either its automated eNote certification process, or its
elements.label: text

elements.text: manual eNote certification process to Seller/Servicer. Freddie Mac will make that
elements.label: text

elements.text: determination at the time of the Seller/Servicer’s approval to sell eMortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Automated eNote certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac and Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodians provide automated
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote certification services for eNotes delivered to Freddie Mac. Such automated eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification will occur after the eNotes are in Freddie Mac’s or the Freddie Mac￾approved third-party eNote custodian’s eNote Vault System and Seller/Servicer has
elements.label: text

elements.text: provided all required loan data to Loan Selling Advisor.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Manual eNote certification
elements.label: text

elements.text: In the event Freddie Mac or a Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian
elements.label: text

elements.text: determines that Seller/Servicer must use the manual eNote certification process, in
elements.label: text

elements.text: addition to applicable Note verification requirements as identified in Section 6304.3(a),
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac or a Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian must verify that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Loan Selling Advisor properly identifies eMortgages by confirming:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The term “EMTG” is on the Form 1034E in Loan Selling Advisor or on a printed
elements.label: text

elements.text: version of Form 1034E; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ The “Enote Indicator” column in the data export from Loan Selling Advisor is
elements.label: text

elements.text: “true”; or
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ A check mark is in a column denoted with or labeled with a lower case “e,” in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: certification screens in Loan Selling Advisor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The MIN in Loan Selling Advisor is the same as the MIN on the eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Tamper Seal on the eNote matches the Tamper Seal stored in the MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: eRegistry
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Freddie Mac is the Controller of the eNote and Freddie Mac or a Freddie Mac￾approved third-party eNote custodian is the Location of the eNote in the MERS
elements.label: text

elements.text: eRegistry
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the eNote is viewed in the eNote Vault System, there is a representation of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower’s typed name and Electronic Signature (which may or may not include a
elements.label: text

elements.text: time and date stamp), and the Borrower’s legal signing capacity, if signing other than
elements.label: text

elements.text: in an individual capacity
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the eNote is viewed in the eNote Vault System, MERSCORP Holdings, Inc., a
elements.label: text

elements.text: Delaware corporation, has been named as the “Operator of the Registry” in the eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: Uniform Instrument
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The eNote is a valid Version 1.02 MISMO Category 1 SMART Document; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ When the Borrower’s holographic signature is not consistent with a typed or printed
elements.label: text

elements.text: name below the signature line on the Note or in Loan Selling Advisor, a signature
elements.label: text

elements.text: affidavit or name affidavit is not required for eMortgages since the eClosing System
elements.label: text

elements.text: involves authentication of the Borrower and has an associated audit trail
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer is responsible for resolving discrepancies before certification can be
elements.label: text

elements.text: completed. If the eNote does not meet certification requirements and the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: has been unable to resolve outstanding issues, the eNote will be transferred back to the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer and purchase by Freddie Mac will not occur.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) eMortgage post-closing corrections and other requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Major corrections
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer discovers a major error or omission (such as loan amount, Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: Date, Floor Rate Percent, Ceiling Rate Percent, etc.) that affects the enforceability of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote, Electronic Security Instrument or other Electronic Mortgage File Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: (“Major Error”) after closing, but before delivery to Freddie Mac, Seller/Servicer may
elements.label: text

elements.text: correct such Major Error by having the Borrower re-execute the eNote, Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument or other Electronic Mortgage File Document, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Minor corrections
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Seller/Servicer discovers a scrivener’s or ministerial error or omission (such as
elements.label: text

elements.text: misspelled City, State or Street names, etc.) that does not adversely affect the
elements.label: text

elements.text: enforceability of the eNote, Electronic Security Instrument or Electronic Mortgage File
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents or affecting the First Lien status of the eMortgage (“Minor Error”) in any
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote, Electronic Security Instrument or Electronic Mortgage File Documents after
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing, but before delivery to Freddie Mac, Seller/Servicer may correct any such Minor
elements.label: text

elements.text: Error using the procedures and processes below:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer may, pursuant to a limited power of attorney or closing
elements.label: text

elements.text: compliance authorization agreement duly executed by the Borrower:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Make corrections only to a Seller/Servicer certified paper copy of the eNote,
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic Security Instrument or Electronic Mortgage File Document marked
elements.label: text

elements.text: “Copy,” as applicable; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Execute an affidavit similar to the sample provided in Exhibit 45 and insert the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable information on a form similar to the sample provided in Exhibit 46
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Store Electronic copies of such documents in Seller/Servicer’s eNote Vault
elements.label: text

elements.text: System or eStorage System
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Deliver such documents to Freddie Mac’s or a Freddie Mac-approved third-party
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote custodian’s eNote Vault System as Electronic Records, in an Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: format acceptable to Freddie Mac for storage with related eNotes; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Deliver a paper copy of such documents to the Borrower on the day such
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents are executed
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer represents and warrants to, and agrees and covenants with Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac that eNotes, Electronic Security Instruments and Electronic Mortgage File
elements.label: text

elements.text: Documents corrected as set forth herein are accurate, valid, effective, enforceable,
elements.label: text

elements.text: and in First Lien position and in compliance with all applicable laws, rules, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: regulations, including, without limitation, E-SIGN and the UETA, as enacted in the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable jurisdiction
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The Seller/Servicer may not use this procedure to correct any eNote, Electronic
elements.label: text

elements.text: Security Instrument or Electronic Mortgage File Document after the eMortgage has
elements.label: text

elements.text: been delivered to Freddie Mac, unless the Seller/Servicer obtains Freddie Mac’s prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: written approval
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Minor Error correction process in this Section 1402.3(c)(ii) is provided as a
elements.label: text

elements.text: convenience for the Seller/Servicer and not as legal advice. The Seller/Servicer is (and
elements.label: text

elements.text: remains) solely responsible for the validity, effectiveness and enforceability of any eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: or other Electronic Record and the First Lien status of the eMortgage corrected in this
elements.label: text

elements.text: manner. The Seller/Servicer must confer with its legal counsel before using this process
elements.label: text

elements.text: in any State. Freddie Mac assumes no liability for the correction process used by the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)Electronic Record image quality
elements.label: text

elements.text: If an Electronic Record is delivered to Freddie Mac’s or a Freddie Mac-approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: third-party eNote custodian’s eNote Vault System and Freddie Mac or the Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: approved third-party eNote custodian, as applicable, determines that the image quality of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Electronic Record is unacceptable, Freddie Mac may require the Seller/Servicer to
elements.label: text

elements.text: provide an Electronic copy of the Electronic Record that is acceptable to Freddie Mac or
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Freddie Mac-approved third-party eNote custodian, as applicable.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)eMortgage file documentation management
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Mortgage File Documents for eMortgages delivered may contain paper and
elements.label: text

elements.text: Electronic documents, so long as Seller/Servicer:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Maintains Records in the Mortgage file, eNote Vault System, and/or eStorage System
elements.label: text

elements.text: that cross-reference the paper and Electronic documents, and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Promptly delivers the Electronic Custodial Documents to Freddie Mac, its designee or
elements.label: text

elements.text: through restricted access

elements.text: (v) Selling eNotes with or without recourse

elements.text: Unless the Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac have agreed (or subsequently agree) in the

elements.text: Mac are sold by the Seller/Servicer “without recourse,” as that term is used and described

elements.text: in Article 3 of the UCC and Section 6201.2(b)(ii) of the Guide. Despite the sale of eNotes

elements.text: being sold “without recourse” to Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer remains responsible and
elements.label: text

elements.text: liable for:

elements.text: (i) All of its representations, warranties, covenants and agreements under

elements.text: or indemnification agreements applicable to any eMortgage sold hereunder, and

elements.text: requirements applicable to paper promissory notes and other negotiable instruments.

elements.text: (vi)Title insurance requirements

elements.text: Despite the transfer warranties or anything else contained in Section 3-416 of Article 3

elements.text: of the UCC, the Seller/Servicer, as transferor of each eNote transferred to Freddie Mac

elements.text: for consideration, represents and warrants to, and agrees and covenants with, Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac, as transferee, and to Freddie Mac’s successors and/or assigns, as successor
elements.label: text

elements.text: transferees, that:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each eNote is maintained in an eNote Vault System that satisfies the requirements of:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Section 16(b) and (c) of the UETA
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ Section 201(b) and (c) of E-SIGN; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ❑ eNote Vault System requirements specified in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The transferor is duly authorized to transfer each eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The transferor is a person or an organization entitled to enforce each eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ All Electronic Signatures attached to or logically associated with each eNote, and in
elements.label: text

elements.text: connection with any prior authorization to transfer, are authentic, authorized, genuine

elements.text: and enforceable against the party purporting to have electronically signed the eNote
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each eNote is not subject to a defense or a claim of recoupment of any party that can
elements.label: text

elements.text: be asserted against the transferor
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ The transferor has no knowledge of any insolvency proceeding commenced or
elements.label: text

elements.text: threatened with respect to the Borrower on each eNote; and
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Each eNote has at all times been maintained by a person identified as the Holder: (a)
elements.label: text

elements.text: to whom the eNote was issued or transferred; and (b) asserting Control of the eNote,
elements.label: text

elements.text: or that Holder’s designated custodian
elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: of the eNotes sold hereunder in good faith may recover from the Seller/Servicer (as

elements.text: transferor) damages for breach of any of the representations, warranties, agreements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: covenants set forth in this Section 1402.3(c)(vii), in an amount equal to the loss suffered

elements.text: as a result of the breach, but not more than the unpaid principal balance of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: applicable eNote at the time of any such breach, plus expenses, loss of interest, and

elements.text: other amounts secured by the related eMortgage that are incurred as a result of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: breach.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The foregoing is not in limitation of any remedies of Freddie Mac contained in other
elements.label: text

elements.text: chapters of the Guide or available under applicable law.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Seller/Servicer and Freddie Mac agree that under no circumstances shall the transfer

elements.text: warranties or any conditions precedent to enforcement of such transfer warranties

elements.text: contained in Section 3-416 of Article 3 of the UCC (or in the UCC as enacted in the State

elements.text: in which the Mortgaged Premises is located) apply to eNotes sold to Freddie Mac.

elements.text: (viii)eMortgage representations, warranties and covenants

elements.text: In addition to making all applicable representations and warranties elsewhere in the

elements.text: Guide and in the Seller/Servicer’s other Purchase Documents, the Seller/Servicer further

elements.text: represents and warrants to, and agrees and covenants with, Freddie Mac, with respect to
elements.label: text

elements.text: each and every eMortgage (both Retail Mortgages and Wholesale Home Mortgages) sold

elements.text: to Freddie Mac, that:

elements.text: Form”) in advance of being presented with any Electronic Record to be electronically

elements.text: signed
elements.label: text

elements.text: documents comply with:

elements.text: and

elements.text: (d) Use of eMortgage Warehouse Lender
elements.label: text

elements.text: This Section 1402.3(d) supplements and amends Chapter 6305 by setting forth the following
elements.label: text

elements.text: requirements for Seller/Servicers and their Warehouse Lenders with respect to eMortgages:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ A Freddie Mac-approved Seller/Servicer that is also approved by Freddie Mac to
elements.label: text

elements.text: originate and sell eMortgages to Freddie Mac, may enter into an eMortgage Warehousing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agreement with Freddie Mac-approved eMortgage Warehouse Lenders to facilitate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: closing or purchase of eMortgages, and may grant the Warehouse Lender a lien, interest
elements.label: text

elements.text: and/or security interest in such eMortgages and related eNote.
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ If a Seller/Servicer wishes to use Warehouse Lenders that are not Freddie Mac-approved

elements.text: described in Section 1402.6(a) to obtain Freddie Mac approval, before Seller/Servicer can

elements.text: ■ The requirements applicable to Seller/Servicers and Warehouse Lenders for the delivery
elements.label: text

elements.text: of Pledged eMortgages are set forth in Section 1402.6

elements.label: text

elements.text: https://sf.freddiemac.com/working-with-us/electronic-loan-documents/emortgage-enote￾warehouse-lenders)

elements.text: (e) Post purchase support
elements.label: text

elements.text: A Seller/Servicer that creates, stores or transfers an eNote purchased by Freddie Mac must
elements.label: text

elements.text: cooperate with Freddie Mac in all activities necessary to enforce the Security Instrument and
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote. Promptly upon request by Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer must provide or, if

elements.text: applicable, cause its third-party System Provider to provide to Freddie Mac an affidavit or

elements.label: text

elements.text: Records in the Mortgage file and other Custodial Documents in connection with the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage. The affidavit or certification must be in a form appropriate to ensure

elements.text: UETA

elements.label: text

elements.label: text

elements.text: of the data; and

elements.text: In addition to the requirements set forth in Chapter 2202, the Freddie Mac-approved third￾party eNote custodian covenants to Freddie Mac that it will do the following:

elements.label: text
