elements.text: This section contains requirements related to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing performing eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Servicing non-performing eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Mortgage File Documents management
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Transfer of Servicing involving eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Special eMortgage Servicing representations and warranties
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Voluntary eMortgage repurchase request
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ New York CEMA Refinance of New York eMortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: (a) Servicing eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: The Servicing requirements of this chapter are applicable only to the Servicing of
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgages. Seller/Servicers must not apply any of the requirements set forth in this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: to a non-eMortgage, but rather, should continue Servicing such Mortgages in accordance
elements.label: text

elements.text: with all other applicable Servicing requirements of the Servicing Contract, the Guide and
elements.label: text

elements.text: any other applicable Purchase Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Becoming an eMortgage Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: To become an eMortgage Servicer for Freddie Mac, the Seller/Servicer must contact its
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac account representative to determine its eligibility and obtain Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: prior written approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s approval of a Seller/Servicer to service eMortgages for Freddie Mac does
elements.label: text

elements.text: not necessarily mean that the Seller/Servicer will also be approved as a Seller of
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgages to Freddie Mac or vice versa. The approval process to sell eMortgages is set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in Sections 1402.1 through 1402.3.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: An approved Servicer of eMortgages may only engage in a Transfer of Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: involving eMortgages in accordance with Section 1402.5(e)(i).
elements.label: text

elements.text: To obtain and maintain approval to service eMortgages, a Seller/Servicer shall at all
elements.label: text

elements.text: times meet the following requirements or use the services of a Freddie Mac-approved
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage Servicer as a Servicing Agent:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Be a MERS eDelivery and MERS eRegistry participant
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Have a Freddie Mac-reviewed eNote Vault System. (See Section 1402.2 for eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vault System requirements, eNote Vault initial and ongoing review requirements and
elements.label: text

elements.text: notice of significant change requirements.)
elements.label: text

elements.text: The eNote Vault System must have the ability to:
elements.label: text

elements.text: a. Maintain a Copy of the Authoritative Copy of the eNote and its Tamper Evident
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seal for the life of the eMortgage plus seven years
elements.label: text

elements.text: b. Identify and track all eMortgages that the Seller/Servicer services for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: c. Record all status changes and required actions that occur during the life of the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage in the MERS® eRegistry
elements.label: text

elements.text: d. Accept an offer of change of Control from Freddie Mac, in the event of a
elements.label: text

elements.text: foreclosure or other Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters with respect to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: e. Create an offer of change of Control to Freddie Mac, in the event of termination
elements.label: text

elements.text: of a foreclosure or other Freddie Mac Default Legal Matters with respect to an
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage, as applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: f. Confirm that the MERS eRegistry accurately reflects the Controller and Location
elements.label: text

elements.text: at all times
elements.label: text

elements.text: g. Accept a transfer of the Authoritative Copy of the eNote from Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote Vault System using MERS eDelivery, in the event of an eMortgage
elements.label: text

elements.text: repurchase by the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: h. Securely store Electronic copies of Mortgage File Documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Note: A Seller/Servicer can store all Electronic Mortgage File Documents in its
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote Vault System, if the eNote Vault System allows such storage. If the eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: Vault System does not permit storage of Electronic Mortgage File Documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: other than the eNote, the Seller/Servicer must have a secure eStorage System for
elements.label: text

elements.text: storing such documents.
elements.label: text

elements.text: See Section 1402.3(c)(iv) for eStorage System requirements.
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3. Have written eMortgage Servicing policies and procedures in place
elements.label: text

elements.text: 4. Meet any other eMortgage requirements imposed by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac may exercise any and all of its rights under the Guide and applicable
elements.label: text

elements.text: Purchase Documents, including, without limitation, the right set forth in Section
elements.label: text

elements.text: 3603.1(a) to terminate for cause a Seller/Servicer’s Servicing Contract, in whole or in
elements.label: text

elements.text: part, if the Seller/Servicer fails to continue to meet the eligibility requirements set forth
elements.label: text

elements.text: above, including, but not limited to, the Seller/Servicer’s MERS eRegistry participation
elements.label: text

elements.text: (or their Servicing Agent’s MERS eRegistry participation, if applicable) being
elements.label: text

elements.text: terminated or if the Seller/Servicer is unable to record status changes and required
elements.label: text

elements.text: actions that occur during the life of the eMortgage in the MERS eRegistry.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon such termination, the Seller/Servicer agrees to facilitate a Transfer of Servicing of
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac’s eMortgages to Freddie Mac’s designated eMortgage Servicer at
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer’s own expense.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Becoming an eMortgage Servicing Agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Subject to Chapter 8102, a Seller/Servicer approved to service eMortgages for Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac may, as a Master Servicer, engage a Servicer as its Servicing Agent to service such
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgages.
elements.label: text

elements.text: An eMortgage Servicing Agent is not required to obtain separate approval to service
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgages, provided the eMortgage Servicing Agent uses the Master Servicer’s Freddie
elements.label: text

elements.text: Mac-reviewed eNote Vault System and Servicing staff to record status changes and
elements.label: text

elements.text: required actions that occur during the life of the eMortgage in the MERS eRegistry and
elements.label: text

elements.text: to create/accept offers of change of Control and Location requests, as applicable, during
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Servicing of an eMortgage.
elements.label: text

elements.text: However, if an eMortgage Servicing Agent intends on using its own eNote Vault
elements.label: text

elements.text: System, the eMortgage Servicing Agent must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Obtain separate approval to service eMortgages for Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure its eNote Vault System complies with the requirements of this chapter
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Have written eMortgage Servicing policies and procedures in place
elements.label: text

elements.text: Regardless of the eNote Vault System used by the eMortgage Servicing Agent, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Master Servicer and the eMortgage Servicing Agent must have obtained Freddie Mac’s
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval of their fully executed Form 479A, Single-Family Servicing Agent
elements.label: text

elements.text: Certification and Agreement, with an effective date on or after September 18, 2018.
elements.label: text

elements.text: For a Master Servicer and its eMortgage Servicing Agent that have been approved by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac based on their submission of Form 479A with an effective date prior to
elements.label: text

elements.text: September 18, 2018, the Master Servicer and its eMortgage Servicing Agent must
elements.label: text

elements.text: execute a new Form 479A and submit it to Freddie Mac and obtain Freddie Mac’s prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: approval in accordance with Section 8102.1.
elements.label: text

elements.text: Consistent with Section 8201.1, a Master Servicer may use only one eMortgage Servicing
elements.label: text

elements.text: Agent for eMortgages being serviced under a particular Freddie Mac Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: number at any one time. Freddie Mac’s approval of an eMortgage Servicing Agent is
elements.label: text

elements.text: limited to the Master Servicer designated on the approved Form 479A. Before an
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage Servicing Agent can subservice eMortgages for another Master Servicer, a
elements.label: text

elements.text: separate fully executed Form 479A must be submitted to obtain Freddie Mac’s prior
elements.label: text

elements.text: written approval.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (b) Servicing performing eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) eMortgage recast modification requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon a recast modification of an eMortgage under Section 8103.3(d), the Seller/Servicer
elements.label: text

elements.text: must update the MERS® eRegistry to provide notice of the modification agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (A)Paper modification agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the modification of an eMortgage is memorialized in a paper modification
elements.label: text

elements.text: agreement, the Seller/Servicer must adhere to the requirements in Section 9206.4(b).
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the Document Custodian receiving the original paper modification agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: requires a copy of the eNote, the Seller/Servicer must print a paper copy from the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eNote Vault and deliver it to the Document Custodian. The Seller/Servicer may not
elements.label: text

elements.text: convert the eNote into a paper note.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (B) eModification Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: If the modification of an eMortgage is memorialized in an eModification, the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer must comply with the following requirements:
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Create and execute the eModification Agreement on a SMART Document version
elements.label: text

elements.text: that meets the MERS eRegistry requirements
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Register the eModification Agreement with the MERS eRegistry to associate the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eModification Agreement with the previously registered eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that the MIN assigned to the eModification Agreement is the same as the
elements.label: text

elements.text: MIN assigned to the previously registered eNote
elements.label: text

elements.text: ■ Ensure that the signing platform has a robust audit trail of all key events starting
elements.label: text

elements.text: from the creation of the eModification Agreement through and including the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Borrower and Seller/Servicer execution that the Seller/Servicer can reproduce
elements.label: text

elements.text: upon request by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: (ii) Assumption of eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: Upon an assumption (with or without a release of liability) of an eMortgage under
elements.label: text

elements.text: Chapter 8406, the Seller/Servicer must update the MERS eRegistry to provide notice of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the assumption agreement.
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iii)eMortgage payoffs
elements.label: text

elements.text: In States that require the return of a paper Note marked “paid-in-full” upon payoff of
elements.label: text

elements.text: an eMortgage, the Seller/Servicer must:
elements.label: text

elements.text: 1. Provide the Borrower with a paper copy of the eNote (marked “Copy” and
elements.label: text

elements.text: “paid-in-full”), and
elements.label: text

elements.text: 2. Include a letter to the Borrower that explains they are receiving a paper copy of
elements.label: text

elements.text: the eNote, originally registered in the MERS eRegistry, and that the
elements.label: text

elements.text: Seller/Servicer has caused the eNote to be de-activated on the MERS eRegistry
elements.label: text

elements.text: due to the eMortgage having been paid in full
elements.label: text

elements.text: (iv)Conversion of the eNote to a paper Note
elements.label: text

elements.text: No party, including the Seller/Servicer, may convert an eNote owned by Freddie Mac
elements.label: text

elements.text: into a paper-based Note. A Seller/Servicer that needs to convert an eNote owned by
elements.label: text

elements.text: Freddie Mac into a paper-based Note must contact
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage_Team@FreddieMac.com for Freddie Mac’s review.
elements.label: text

elements.text: The one exception to this requirement is when a Seller/Servicer is Servicing an
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage originated in New York and the Borrower later wishes to refinance the
elements.label: text

elements.text: eMortgage using the New York Consolidation, Modification and Extension Agreement
elements.label: text

elements.text: (NYCEMA) process and documents. In such case, the Seller/Servicer must comply
elements.label: text

elements.text: with the requirements in Section 1402.5(h).
elements.label: text

elements.text: (c) Servicing non-performing eMortgages
elements.label: text

elements.text: (i) Electronic Payment Deferral documents
elements.label: text

elements.text: If an eMortgage is eligible for Payment Deferral in accordance with the requirements set
elements.label: text

elements.text: forth in Chapter 9203, and if it is memorialized in a paper Payment Deferral Agreement,
elements.label: text

elements.text: the Seller/Servicer must comply with the requirements set forth in Section 9203.4(f).
